strengths: types.ts has zero imports — provider-agnostic constraint satisfied exactly (no @anthropic-ai/sdk or @tavily/core)
registry.ts imports only the local Tool type; clean separation between contract and provider coupling
errorCode typed as string (open, not a closed enum) per QUALITY BAR open-question guidance, so T3 can populate it freely
toAnthropicToolDefs uses object destructuring to map inputSchema -> input_schema while structurally excluding execute/riskTier/inputSchema — no leakage of internal fields into the Anthropic def
All 5 registry tests pass; tests assert both behavior (resolve/drop/empty) and negative shape (no property leakage)

issues.description: ToolEvent implemented as a discriminated union where the 'reasoning' variant permits only {phase, text}, whereas the plan (step 3) specified a flat type and stated 'toolName optional for reasoning events'. The union is better-typed and no committed consumer (T4 emits {phase:"reasoning", text}) needs toolName on reasoning events, so nothing breaks — but it is a literal deviation from the spec'd shape, noted not blocking.
'Tests written before implementation' (QUALITY BAR must-have) cannot be verified from the squashed SHA range; tests exist and pass, but TDD ordering is unconfirmed from history.
